The device identifier for the source device generating the log.
The timestamp of the logged event.
The device name or hostname of the logging device.
The numeric category or type of the log event.
The priority or severity level of the log.
The software or firmware version of the logging system.
The source IP address of the event or device.
The module or subsystem within the Themis tunnel generating the log.
The result code or status of the action.
The username or account identity executing the operation.
The display message containing a human-readable description of the event.
The firewall log index or reference number.
Display name of the device or application
Outcome or status of the action
Device identifier for the network device generating the log, useful for device-level grouping and entity identification.
Timestamp indicating when the log event occurred, crucial for temporal alignment and event sequencing.
Device name, providing a human-readable identifier for the source device to support entity alignment.
Numeric log type indicator, used for event categorization and filtering during log correlation.
Priority level of the log event, supporting severity-based filtering and incident prioritization.
Software or protocol version string, useful for matching logs from compatible systems and tracking version changes.
Source IP address identifying the origin of the traffic or request, key for network linkage and threat attribution.
Module or feature name (e.g., blacklist), enabling module-specific event grouping and analysis.
Action performed (e.g., display, restore), providing the operation type for process lineage and event correlation.
Outcome of the action, indicating success or failure for event completion tracking.
Username of the account performing the action, essential for user activity tracing and access auditing.
Human-readable display message providing additional context about the event, useful for log enrichment and investigation.
Firewall log indicator or related flag, supporting firewall event correlation and linkage.
